skill.name: requirement-translator
skill.description: Translates vague, subjective, or incomplete user requests into structured Chinese requirement specifications with goals, constraints, acceptance criteria, risks, and priorities.
skill.summary: Turn unclear requests into executable Chinese requirement specifications.
skill.author: Barry-Liu-888

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a C2-like token, a weak-crypto keyword, and high entropy. Manual review found these are false positives from a fenced output template, the label AC2, YAML description text, inline Markdown, and Chinese-language content. No executable code, prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks at SKILL.md lines 36, 66, and 78. Lines 36 and 66 delimit a fenced Markdown output template, and line 78 uses inline Markdown around a Chinese inference marker. No shell command or executable instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown formatting and template text, not Ruby or shell execution. There is no command name, interpolation, or runtime context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword Match in Acceptance Criteria Label
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 56 as a C2 keyword. The text is the label AC2 inside an acceptance-criteria template, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is a requirements document template. No network endpoint, beaconing logic, or remote-control instruction appears near the label.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Metadata
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The line is a YAML description field and does not reference cryptography or implement an algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is metadata for the skill description. There is no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher configuration, or security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy from Human-Readable Content
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 1 for high entropy. Manual review shows a normal Markdown skill file with Chinese text and no binary, encrypted, or obfuscated payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The complete file is readable Markdown with YAML front matter and a requirements workflow. The entropy signal is plausibly caused by multilingual text, not obfuscation.

content.user_title: Translate Vague Requests into Clear Specs
content.value_statement: Unclear requests slow down planning and cause avoidable rework. This skill turns vague input into structured Chinese specifications that Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can act on.
actual_capabilities: Identifies core goals, ambiguity, missing information, and implicit assumptions.
Formats requests into a Chinese requirement specification with seven named sections.
Adds functional requirements, technical constraints, design constraints, and acceptance criteria.
Documents boundary conditions, exception handling, and P0 to P2 priorities.
Provides recommendation, risk notes, and an implementation sequence after the specification.
limitations: Outputs Chinese requirement translations only, based on the skill instructions.
Does not validate requirements against real systems, codebases, budgets, or timelines.
Requires user confirmation when critical missing information cannot be inferred.
Does not implement the translated requirement or generate production code.

use_cases.title: Clarify Product Requests
use_cases.description: Convert a broad feature idea into goals, requirements, acceptance criteria, and priority levels.

use_cases.title: Prepare Development Tasks
use_cases.description: Turn informal stakeholder language into implementation-ready requirements for an engineering backlog.

use_cases.title: Improve AI Task Prompts
use_cases.description: Rewrite an unclear AI instruction into a precise specification before asking an assistant to execute it.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a Simple Request
prompt_templates.prompt: 请把这个需求转译为结构化需求规格书：做一个好看的登录页面。

prompt_templates.title: Translate a Feature Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: 需求转译：我们想让用户更容易找到订单问题，并减少客服咨询。

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Technical Task
prompt_templates.prompt: 请明确这个需求：优化搜索功能，让结果更准确，速度也更快。请列出推断、待确认问题和验收标准。

prompt_templates.title: Create an Execution-Ready Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: 请将以下复杂需求转译为可执行规格，并补充推荐方案、风险提示和实施顺序：为企业后台增加权限管理，支持多角色、多部门和审计记录。

output_examples.input: 做一个好用的报表页面。
output_examples.output: The skill would define the reporting page goal, list vague terms like good and useful, infer likely filters and export needs, and create acceptance criteria for loading, empty states, and responsive layout.

output_examples.input: 帮我优化用户注册流程。
output_examples.output: The skill would separate conversion goals, required form behavior, technical constraints, design constraints, error handling, and P0 to P2 priorities.

output_examples.input: 这个需求不够清楚：让搜索更智能。
output_examples.output: The skill would identify missing ranking rules, data sources, latency targets, fallback behavior, and confirmation questions before implementation.

best_practices: Provide the original request and any known business context before using the skill.
Review every item marked as inferred before sending the specification to an implementation agent.
Add measurable targets for speed, quality, compatibility, and acceptance criteria whenever possible.
anti_patterns: Do not treat inferred requirements as approved decisions without stakeholder review.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for legal, security, or compliance validation.
Do not ask it to implement code after translation without a separate implementation request.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a structured Chinese requirement specification with goals, requirements, constraints, acceptance criteria, risks, and priorities.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a request is vague, subjective, incomplete, or not ready for implementation.

faq.question: Does it ask follow-up questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists questions when key information is missing and cannot be reasonably inferred.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The translated specification can make requests clearer for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it support English output?
faq.answer: The skill instructions require Chinese output for translated requirements.

faq.question: Does it create implementation code?
faq.answer: No. It prepares requirements and execution guidance, but it does not implement the solution.
